sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.19 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$p$be a prime, and${ F}_p$the class of finitely generated groups acting faithfully on a$p$-regu\-lar rooted tree by finite automata. Any group in${ F}_p$is residually-$p$(residually in the class of finite$p$-groups) and has word problem that is solvable in (at worst) exponential time. There exist therefore groups that are residually-$p$, have a solvable word problem, and do not belong to${ F}_p...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
